items.title: SA locks in AFL Gather Round until 2029 but no decision on venues

children.content: The AFL's Gather Round will stay in South Australia until 2029 under a new three-year partnership. It will include a parade and a "feature match" but no decision has been made on venues.

gallery.altText: Children wearing AFL jumpers run through a large blue banner saying GATHER ROUND TO STAY IN SA
Two men sign papers on a black table while children wearing AFL jumpers look on within a stadium
A football oval with workers putting up temporary seating and scaffolding.

thumbnail.altText: Children wearing AFL jumpers run through a large blue banner saying GATHER ROUND TO STAY IN SA
Two men sign papers on a black table while children wearing AFL jumpers look on within a stadium
A football oval with workers putting up temporary seating and scaffolding.

items.title: Fraudsters jailed for stealing millions from 'older and vulnerable' victims

children.content: Two men who "exploited the vulnerability" of multiple victims by swindling millions of dollars in a fraudulent property investment scheme have been jailed for the "undoubtedly serious" offending.

image.altText: A composite image of a bald man and a man with a beard

items.title: Adelaide defence lawyer pleads not guilty to sexual offences

children.content: Andrew Graham enters not guilty pleas to two counts of rape and one charge of compelling a person to sexually manipulate an offender.

image.altText: Andrew Graham is speaking outside a sandstone coloured court building

items.title: 'Inspirational' Dawson stars as Crows send premiership statement

children.content: Jordan Dawson plays one of the best quarters of his career as Adelaide claims a 57-point win over the Western Bulldogs at Docklands.

image.altText: Jordan Dawson celebrates a goal with Adelaide teammates

items.title: Bakery trains staff to assess counterfeit notes amid rise in use in SA

children.content: An Adelaide business owner who has been targeted by counterfeiters about a dozen times is warning others to be vigilant, as SA Police warn the number of incidents has increased in the state in the past year.

image.altText: A close-up of a hand holding a $50 note and shining an blue UV light on the clear strip.

items.title: Consumer watchdog warns real estate agents must not misrepresent homes

children.content: Real estate agents selling homes must not misrepresent information about the builder or serious defects, SA's consumer watchdog has said, after eight builders were hit with public warning notices over their conduct in the state.

gallery.altText: A skirting board with a gap to a door frame

thumbnail.altText: A skirting board with a gap to a door frame

items.title: Brother and sister from remote SA town receive King's Birthday Honours

children.content: A brother and sister who grew up near the rural town of Lock on the Eyre Peninsula both received honours over the King's Birthday long weekend.

image.altText: A woman stands next to a sign in a rural area. A paddock is behind her.

items.title: Predictive text in 'demonstrable decline' with AI-based language models

children.content: If internet chatter is to be believed, the intuitiveness and efficiency of predictive text and autocorrect have fallen off a cliff in recent years. But while researchers agree there has been a demonstrable decline in quality, the increasing sophistication of what phones can do is also making errors more prominent.

image.altText: a close up of a person holding a mobile phone and texting

items.title: Woman struck by garbage truck among three fatalities on SA roads

children.content: Three people die in separate crashes on South Australian roads in one day, including a 23-year-old woman who was struck by a rubbish truck in Adelaide's north-east.

image.altText: A garbage truck next to a green wheelie bin

items.title: NDIA employee charged over alleged $5m fraud plot against NDIS

children.content: Australian Federal Police charge a 48-year-old Adelaide woman over an alleged plot to steal $5 million from the National Disability Insurance Service and a disability services provider.

image.altText: Two police officers walk with a woman wearing a red tracksuit and bag on her head to a car with FEDERAL POLICE on the door

items.title: Seized greyhounds looked like 'RSPCA cases', kennel owner says

children.content: A dog kennel owner is searching for answers after Greyhound Racing SA put two dogs that appeared malnourished and one with an open wound "you could stick your finger into" in her care.
Warning: This article contains images and content that some readers may find distressing.

image.altText: A woman in a pink sweatshirt patting a greyhound.

items.title: Beekeepers call for access to new biopesticide to target varroa mite

children.content: A new biopesticide could help battle growing resistance to varroa mite treatments in Australia.

image.altText: a man in beekeeping outfit stares at testing tube

items.title: 'Keyboard warriors orchestrating hate': Northern Ireland leader hits out at Musk and Robinson

children.content: Billionaire Elon Musk and far-right activist Tommy Robinson have seized upon the alleged stabbing of a man in Belfast by a Sudanese immigrant to push an anti-immigration agenda.

children.content: The family of a Northern Irish man who lost an eye in a knife attack has appealed for calm ​after anti-immigrant violence in Belfast.

children.content: As UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer fights for his political life, the country is bracing for what could be a yet another summer of unrest.

items.title: 'Fugitive' ex-bikie detained in Bali found hiding in private jet toilet

children.content: Former bikie boss Angelo Pandeli was attempting to fly to Africa on a fake Brazilian passport when Indonesian immigration authorities apprehended him over an Interpol notice.

items.title: Prosecutors cast LA arson suspect as dejected loner, angry at society

children.content: Jonathan Rinderknecht's attorney countered that a hilltop blaze was triggered by fireworks, not his client, and that the massive Palisades inferno that killed 12 people was a separate fire set by others.

items.title: State disability ministers lash NDIS overhaul

children.content: State and territory disability ministers have accused the Albanese government of pushing on with NDIS reform that does not align with previous agreements.

image.altText: A man in a suit and tie looks sternly ahead.

items.title: World's biggest whale graveyard found in Indian Ocean off Australia

children.content: Hundreds of whale fossils, including those from extinct species new to science, range in age from recent to 5 million years old and are also home to undescribed deep-sea creatures.

image.altText: Underwater vision of a whale fossil on the seafloor.

items.title: Are YouTubers taking over the film industry?

children.content: YouTube is changing the film industry - it's place where young filmmakers can find and build an audience.
More and more YouTube stars are making the jump into mainstream films.
So, are YouTubers are taking over the film industry?

items.title: In Moldova, I met a woman with a dangerous plan to save her life

children.content: On a park bench in Moldova, I meet a Ukrainian refugee who is planning to return home through a war zone for cancer treatment. We talk for hours.

image.altText: A close up of Tatiana, who has short platimun blonde hair.

items.title: Steven Spielberg returns to sci-fi and alien wonder in Disclosure Day

children.content: ET director Steven Spielberg dives back into the extra terrestrial with his latest film while asking 'How do you astonish an audience when anything is possible, and nothing is real?

items.title: Culture in Australia's largest police force 'unacceptable'

children.content: Bullying, discrimination and sexual harassment are occurring at "unacceptable levels" within the NSW Police Force and employees are too afraid to speak up, an independent report has found.

image.altText: Uniformed Police officers survey a busy protest

items.title: Taylor rules out One Nation seat carve-up as party raises $2m

children.content: Opposition leader Angus Taylor says the Coalition has no plan to share the seats the party targets at the next federal election with One Nation as Pauline Hanson's party says it has raised $2 million since launch.

image.altText: An older man wearing a suit gives an impassioned speech.

items.title: Ebola outbreak grows to almost 600 confirmed cases

children.content: In just two weeks the number of confirmed Ebola virus cases has increased more than eightfold, with at least 100 people now dead.

thumbnail.altText: A person in scrubs applies a mask to a person in full PPE.

items.title: Government flags risk of relying on Elon Musk's SpaceX and Starlink

children.content: As tens of thousands of Australians line up to buy shares in Elon Musk's SpaceX blockbuster public offering, Australian government officials are privately flagging the risks posed by the country's growing reliance on its satellite internet service, Starlink.

image.altText: Elon Musk

items.title: 'Very unfair': Collapsed petrol empire lands deal to pay fraction of $13m debt

children.content: The directors of Zoya Investments have agreed to a $2 million settlement to end the liquidator's case against them.

items.title: 'Just chill, relax': Infantino defends World Cup visa issues, ticket prices

children.content: FIFA president Gianni Infantino defends soccer's governing body's handling of visa issues and ticket prices ahead of the World Cup.

articles.title: Injury concern for Socceroos star on eve of World Cup

children.content: Socceroos striker Mo Toure is a no-show at the beginning of a scheduled training session, bringing his fitness into doubt just days out from their World Cup opener against Türkiye.

image.altText: Mo Toure looks sad during a game for the Socceroos.

articles.title: Mexico City welcomes World Cup but reality bites for many

children.content: As Mexicans prepare to host or co-host the FIFA World Cup for a record third time, many locals are frustrated by being locked out of the tournament, while others are more optimistic.

children.content: NSW Police arrested the 42-year-old in Greenacre in Sydney's south-west overnight and he has been charged with 24 offences.

items.title: 'People are just killing themselves': Solariums operating underground

children.content: Illegal solariums are operating across the country, from single machines secretly set up in suburban neighbourhood garages to those hidden in the back of legitimate businesses.
